goal: Train an IsaacLab ActuatorNet (LSTM) from recorded joint-torque trajectories on a Franka Panda, then inject the trained model back into simulation physics. robot_wizard loads the Franka. record_trajectory captures a diverse-motion excitation sequence (duration=120 s, rate_hz=200) and writes it to workspace/calibration/franka_excitation.npz. get_joint_torques reads the actuator torque columns from the running articulation to confirm the recording has non-trivial effort signal. train_actuator_net trains the LSTM actuator model on the recorded data (hidden_dim=32, num_layers=2, num_epochs=200) and returns the checkpoint path. checkpoint_training saves the training state mid-run as a manual tag. load_rl_policy loads the trained ActuatorNet checkpoint back onto the articulation so simulation physics uses the learned motor model. SUCCESS: train_actuator_net returns a non-null checkpoint path and load_rl_policy succeeds. Exercises the actuator-net training pipeline (record_trajectory, get_joint_torques, train_actuator_net, checkpoint_training, load_rl_policy). Realizes RL / sim-to-real engineer persona.
thoughts: Pattern is rl-train / actuator-net. No cube delivery criterion — success is a non-null checkpoint path returned by train_actuator_net plus a successful load_rl_policy injection. verify_args and simulate_args are null.

record_trajectory is run first to generate the training corpus. duration=120 s at rate_hz=200 yields 24000 samples — sufficient for LSTM training on a 7-DOF arm. Diverse motion (random joint-space sinusoids) is required to capture friction, backlash, and inertia nonlinearities.

get_joint_torques is called immediately after sim play to verify that the articulation is producing non-trivial effort signal (non-zero torques). Zero torques indicate the simulation is paused or the articulation has no drives configured — catching this early prevents silent empty-data training.

train_actuator_net takes real_data_path (the .npz from record_trajectory), articulation_path, hidden_dim=32, num_layers=2, num_epochs=200, output_dir. Default hidden_dim=32 is chosen for real-time inference on Jetson; increase to 64 only if residual error is > 0.5 N·m.
checkpoint_training is called after train_actuator_net to persist the optimizer state with tag='post_actuator_net' — useful for resuming if the injection step fails.
load_rl_policy injects the trained checkpoint back onto the articulation via policy_path and observation_keys=['joint_pos', 'joint_vel', 'joint_effort']. action_dim=7 for Franka Panda.
motion_controllers.untested contains direct_joint because the training workflow uses open-loop joint replay; no closed-loop planner is involved.

Distinct from CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (parametric Bayesian calibration of damping/friction), CP-NEW-rsi-from-demos (Reference State Initialization for RL reset), and CP-NEW-dr-curriculum-trainer (domain-randomization curriculum). This is the ONLY canonical that exercises train_actuator_net end-to-end.
code:
# ── Step 1: Load Franka articulation ─────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.0],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Step 2: Record diverse-motion excitation trajectory ───────────────────────
# Play the timeline (e.g. random joint-space sinusoids) before calling record_trajectory.
_traj_result = record_trajectory(
    articulation="/World/Franka",
    duration=120.0,
    output_path="workspace/calibration/franka_excitation.npz",
    rate_hz=200.0,
)
# Returns: {output_path: str, num_samples: int, joints: [str, ...]}
# Verify: num_samples should be ~24000 (120 s × 200 Hz)

# ── Step 3: Confirm non-trivial torque signal ─────────────────────────────────
_torques = get_joint_torques(
    articulation="/World/Franka",
)
# Returns: {joint_name: applied_torque_Nm, ...} for all 7 joints
# GATE: at least one |torque| > 0.01 N·m; if all zeros the articulation drives are off

# ── Step 4: Train ActuatorNet LSTM ────────────────────────────────────────────
_train_result = train_actuator_net(
    real_data_path="workspace/calibration/franka_excitation.npz",
    articulation_path="/World/Franka",
    hidden_dim=32,
    num_layers=2,
    num_epochs=200,
    output_dir="workspace/calibration/franka_actuator_net",
)
# Returns: {checkpoint_path: str, final_loss: float, training_epochs: int,
#           per_joint_rmse: {joint_name: float}}
# SUCCESS criterion: checkpoint_path is non-null string

# ── Step 5: Checkpoint optimizer state ────────────────────────────────────────
checkpoint_training(
    tag="post_actuator_net",
)
# Returns: {checkpoint_file: str, run_id: str, tag: str}

# ── Step 6: Inject trained ActuatorNet into simulation physics ────────────────
load_rl_policy(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    policy_path=_train_result["checkpoint_path"],
    observation_keys=["joint_pos", "joint_vel", "joint_effort"],
    action_dim=7,
)
# Returns: {status: str, loaded_path: str, observation_keys: [str, ...], action_dim: int}
# Simulation now uses the LSTM actuator model for Franka joint dynamics

diagnose_args.success_criterion: train_result.checkpoint_path is not null AND load_status.status == 'ok'

verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16; form-gate ✓; function-gate BLOCKED (requires live Isaac Sim + Franka articulation + running physics simulation to record trajectories; train_actuator_net needs IsaacLab ML backend)
failure_modes: record_trajectory num_samples=0: simulation timeline must be playing when record_trajectory is called; if the stage is paused the PhysX subscription fires zero times and the output .npz is empty — call sim_control(action='play') first
get_joint_torques all-zero: drives are either disabled (physics:jointEnabled=false) or stiffness/damping are zero; call get_drive_gains on panda_joint1 to verify Kp/Kd before recording

train_actuator_net real_data_path: the .npz must contain arrays named joint_positions, joint_velocities, and joint_torques_commanded at consistent length; record_trajectory writes this schema by default but custom replays may use different key names

train_actuator_net num_epochs=200 with hidden_dim=32: on CPU this takes ~8 min; ensure the call does not time out in the MCP session — pass output_dir so partial checkpoints are written every 50 epochs

checkpoint_training without run_id: targets the most-recent active launch_training run; if no training is running the handler returns an error — this is safe to ignore if train_actuator_net completed synchronously

load_rl_policy policy_path mismatch: the checkpoint written by train_actuator_net is a .pt file; if output_dir was changed from the default, substitute the returned checkpoint_path verbatim rather than constructing the path manually
extension_notes: First canonical to exercise train_actuator_net. Distinct from all physics-calibration and DR canonicals — trains a learned LSTM motor model rather than identifying parametric physical constants. Pairs with CP-NEW-physics-calibrate-actuator (complementary approach: parametric vs. neural).
code_template:
robot_wizard(
    robot_name={{primary_robot.class}},
    dest_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    position={{primary_robot.position}},
    orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}},
)

_traj_result = record_trajectory(
    articulation={{primary_robot.path}},
    duration={{excitation.duration_s}},
    output_path={{excitation.data_path}},
    rate_hz={{excitation.record_rate_hz}},
)

_torques = get_joint_torques(
    articulation={{primary_robot.path}},
)

_train_result = train_actuator_net(
    real_data_path={{excitation.data_path}},
    articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    hidden_dim={{training.hidden_dim}},
    num_layers={{training.num_layers}},
    num_epochs={{training.num_epochs}},
    output_dir={{training.output_dir}},
)

checkpoint_training(
    tag={{checkpoint.tag}},
)

load_rl_policy(
    robot_path={{primary_robot.path}},
    policy_path=_train_result["checkpoint_path"],
    observation_keys=["joint_pos", "joint_vel", "joint_effort"],
    action_dim={{policy.action_dim}},
)
